channel.title: Life Log on yexca'Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Life Log on yexca'Blog

item.title: Playing Honkai: Star Rail Again: Thoughts on the Game Assembly Line
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-3.5-flash I recently checked my email and found Sparkle (a character in Honkai: Star Rail) quite interesting, so I hopped back into the game. (Yes, Sparkle. I actually forgot to publish this post after writing it, and as usual, I ended up uninstalling the game after playing for just a few days). Maybe because it’s been a while, but this playthrough felt different. I didn’t feel like a player; instead, I felt like I was trying to …

item.title: 2025 Annual Review
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash The year-end is approaching, and a new year is beginning. Looking back at this year, so many things happened, to the point where I wasn’t sure if I should write about them or if I even could. After much hesitation, on a swing in the park, I finally decided to write it all down. Continuing from my previous 2024 Annual Review , the first half of this year, to be honest, led me to a further extension of theory, followed by a …

item.title: Curating My Music, Preserving My Vibes
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash My blog started getting super dry and dull after I began posting more specialized learning content. Seriously, opening it just screamed ‘study time,’ which built up this mental block against writing. I’ve wanted to post other stuff for ages but never got around to it. Well, thanks to some recent events, I’m gonna give a non-tech post a shot. 「まだ雨はやまない」 On 2025-03-23, I grabbed my first physical album: Shigure Ui’s ‘まだ雨はやまない’. …

item.title: My Personal Tea Journey
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash This article was created and last modified between 2022.10.31 and 2024.09.15. It logs the teas I’ve tasted and my personal take on them during this period. Honestly, towards the later part, I wasn’t really into this hobby anymore, so the logging got pretty casual. But hey, it was a hobby I pursued, and since the article number hit 233, why not drop something that might be a bit interesting? (Thought I’d published this way …

item.title: 2024 Year in Review
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-3-flash-preview Introduction This post is for myself. It’s a 2024 summary, but also a record of my thoughts and journey over the past few years. My writing isn’t the most polished, and I have a lot I want to say but don’t quite know how to synthesize it all. So, here it is. My Journey from 2020 to 2024 Since 2020, I’ve felt time accelerating. Even though I’ve experienced a lot, I haven’t been able to recapture that pre-2020 feeling—the …

item.title: NetEase Cloud Music 2020-2022 Annual Reports
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-3-flash-preview Links View via mobile (tested on iOS). 2020: https://st.music.163.com/c/year2020 2021: https://st.music.163.com/c/year2021 2022: https://st.music.163.com/c/year2022 The Process Searching Google for NetEase Cloud Music Annual Report led me to https://st.music.163.com/c/reportreview2020 (which acts as a portal for the 2020 report when accessed on mobile). By modifying the year in the URL, I found that the 2020-2022 reports …

item.title: RSS
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-3-flash-preview Introduction I first encountered RSS back when I was browsing HACG. By the time I actually understood it, RSS was already on the decline. Projects like RSSHub were keeping the flame alive, but I eventually gave up on it due to frequent connection issues. However, as I’ve discovered more blogs, I’ve returned to RSS to stay updated and keep track of what I’ve read.

item.title: Logging My First Grey Hack Mission (Password Acquisition)
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash Intro Logging my first mission from yesterday. It’s a retelling, so some details might not be exact. Mission Brief Passed a test from some shadowy figure, got HackShop access, and then the first mission dropped. The mission brief: “The client wants the login credentials of the user Daronane. The remote ip of the victim is 54.44.37.52. It’s important that you access the correct machine behind the public ip. The victim’s ip LAN …

item.title: Project Sekai IP Rules
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-3-flash-preview Introduction I can connect to the game directly, but using a game booster or VPN blocks access. Since toggling the VPN every time I launch the game is a hassle and I couldn’t find a fix online, I’m documenting it here. (Note: Using the iOS JP server). Solution By checking the log files, I identified the domain used during the initial connection. Just add the following domain to your DIRECT rules:

item.title: VRChat Auto-Playing Piano in Chinese World
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash I also wrote a model modification tutorial, you can check it out here: https://vrchat.yexca.net/ Intro Recently, a friend told me that pianos in VRChat can play themselves. So I looked into it and wrote this article to document the process. Note: It seems auto-play only works in PC mode. Of course, you can try casting your desktop in VR (doge). Without Using a MIDI File I. Setup To enable automatic piano playback, you’ll need …

item.title: After the Apricot Rain in Yangzhou
item.description: 📢 Because there is only picture, it cannot be translated This article was written by ctxbb

item.title: Hello World！
item.description: Welcome to WordPress. This is your first post. Edit or delete it and start writing.
